name: Saffron Kulfi Pops
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 6-8 quart pan over high heat, stir milk and sugar until simmering.
2. Over medium-high heat, boil the mixture until reduced to 2 cups, 25-35 minutes, stirring often. If the milk threatens to boil over, slide the pan partially off the heat.
3. Let cool; to speed cooling, set the pan in ice water.
4. Place saffron in a small bowl. Add boiling water and stir, letting stand for 5 minutes. Break up threads with a small spoon and scrape the mixture into the warm reduced milk mixture.
5. Set paper cups in a rimmed pan or make cones by cutting 8 pieces of parchment or waxed paper into 7-1/2 inch squares, folding each piece in half to form a triangle, rolling toward one angle, then pressing the opposite side together and taping to hold it closed. Place pointed end down in a slightly taller cup; set cups in a rimmed pan.
6. Divide the milk mixture among cups or cones. Freeze until kulfi is thick but not hard, 1 to 1-1/2 hours. Push an ice cream stick into each container if desired and freeze for an additional 2 hours or longer.
7. To eat, peel off paper. To store, seal kulfi (still in cups or cones) in a large plastic bag and freeze up to 2 weeks.
Per serving: 143 calories; 6 grams protein; 6.1 grams fat; (3.8 grams saturated fat); 17 grams carbohydrates; 90 milligrams sodium; 26 milligrams cholesterol.
